list_items: The initial setup process for new boxes has been added:
Added the ability to define a Peakboard application for a box group and automatically keep the group's boxes up to date.
Updated the box details page to fit the current UI.
Added a message window indicating that a box update has been started.
The memory graph in the box details will now show the devices max memory as max value.
Added an 'Ungroup' button to the set-group dialog.
Added a button to show the WLAN password.
Added box group names to the Designer box-sync response.
Changed the Hub-to-box communication to token-based authentication instead of basic authentication.
Removed the 5 MB upload limit for the on-premise Hub.
Removed the non-functional selection checkboxes from the box overview list view.
Improved the box overview list view so columns have a consistent width across box groups.
Fixed an issue where the magnifier icon appeared across multiple groups, when hovering over a box in the box overview.
Fixed an issue where empty-state texts were shown even when content was available.
Fixed an issue where zero was used as the default value for box function parameters with a minimum value greater than zero.
Fixed an issue where unusable functionalities were shown in the box overview for user groups with restricted access rights.
Fixed an issue that prevented opening the box details page for boxes with an encrypted connection.
Fixed an issue where existing box groups were not shown correctly in the grouping dialog.
Fixed an issue that allowed creating box groups with a name that already existed within the same user group.
Added list management endpoints (add/update/delete) for the Designer.
Added the ability to add a description to lists, shown via an info icon in the list settings.
Added the ability to group user lists for a better overview.
Added a scrollbar to the list explorer.
Added the list name to the delete-list confirmation dialog.
Updated the list import file filter to show text files (*.txt, *.csv) instead of all files.
Improved the list filter dropdown so its options are sorted like the list columns.
Improved handling for large lists. The header row and horizontal scrollbar always stay visible.
Fixed an issue where adding a row did not work and no error was shown when unsaved data could not be committed.
Fixed an issue where the message box was incorrect when switching lists with invalid unsaved changes.
Fixed an issue where the list selection was lost after saving changes in the list settings.
Fixed an issue where inserting integer values into number columns of Hub lists from the runtime did not work.
Fixed an issue where the default value did not work after changing a list column type from number to string.
Fixed an issue where clicking outside the create-list dialog discarded all entries without confirmation.
Fixed an issue where Hub lists allowed column names containing spaces.
Added the ability to rename a variable, including a warning that this may break connections.
Removed the accept/decline buttons for boolean variables. Changes are now saved directly.
Renamed the file manager root folder in the UI to 'Hub File Root'.
Added the user who saved a version to the file version list.
Added extensions to the hub flows.
Added global function triggers with parameters.
Added live updates for the step states and logs.
Added BacNet as usable datasource.
Added a confirm dialog, which includes a warning about the output lists, when deleting a flow project.
Improved Hub Flow output lists, including plain SQL calls and the ability to download flow lists.
Improved visibility of stopped or crashed flow projects.
Fixed an issue where flow step names did not fit in the UI.
Fixed issues with Modbus and OPC UA.
Fixed an issue where writing a ' character to the hub flow output lists was not possible.
Fixed an issue where the order of flows differed from the Peakboard Designer.
Added single sign-on (SSO) support using the Kerberos protocol.
Added the ability to generate a new Hub user group key.
Removed the group key button and dialog from the users page.
Fixed an issue where user management data could not be loaded on fresh installations with a new database.
Added the server time to the logs page.
Added a functionality to group identical log entries.
Cleaned up unnecessary log entries and moved noisy entries to lower categories.
Fixed the sorting of Hub log entries so they are ordered by time, newest first.
Added the ability to configure a custom SMTP server for email notifications in the settings.
Fixed an issue where alert times were shown in UTC instead of the user's local time.
Updated the frontend to Angular 21.
Added the ability to define a test system and synchronize the production database to a test database.
Users are now redirected to the login page when their session expires.
Reworked the Hub Flow, list, and file icons for clearer communication between features.
Cleaned up inconsistent red colors used throughout the Hub.
Removed the unnecessary bottom line in popup dialogs.
Removed a database optimization from update 1.23 that caused issues on some SQL servers.
Fixed an issue where minimizing the left sidebar enlarged the top section.
Fixed an issue where the login button stayed in a loading state after entering invalid credentials.
Fixed an issue where the version was not displayed correctly on the login screen.
Added an MCP server to the Hub API. Lets AI clients access the Hub API calls via the Model Context Protocol.
Added file management endpoints (read, upload, delete) to the API.
Added an API endpoint to retrieve a screenshot from a box.
We fixed an issue where, if there were multiple hubs on a system, API calls could be routed to the wrong hub.
Fixed an issue where the API returned 'Default' as the group for all boxes.
